fault_hypothesis: On line 67, the overlap check does not incorporate the `buffer` variable (which accounts for `_CONFLICT_STRICT` adjacency), so touching-boundary intervals are incorrectly allowed instead of being rejected.

spec.expr: not (end__1 <= s - buffer__1 or start__1 >= e + buffer__1) == (not (end__1 <= s or start__1 >= e))

constraints.intent: When _CONFLICT_STRICT is enabled, the condition must use buffer to detect touching boundaries; without buffer the check is wrong for adjacent intervals.
After each iteration, if _CONFLICT_STRICT is on and the new interval touches an existing one at a boundary, a conflict should have been returned already.

spec.expr: buffer__1 == (_ADJACENCY_BUFFER if _CONFLICT_STRICT else 0) and (_CONFLICT_STRICT == False or buffer__1 > 0)

constraints.intent: buffer__1 must be positive when _CONFLICT_STRICT is True so that the adjacency check can actually widen the conflict zone.
Before end__1 is used in the overlap condition, when strict mode is on the effective end boundary must be extended by buffer__1.
